config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because once you give the collective too much power, individual rights can be easily abused or ignored. In my view, a healthy society protects people first, then asks them to contribute to the common good within clear limits. From my perspective, the best balance is not “society over individual,” but strong individual rights with responsibilities to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” hak minoritas dan kebebasan dasar sering jadi korban. Namun, hak individu juga bukan kartu bebas tanpa batas; ada situasi nyata seperti kesehatan publik, keselamatan, dan penegakan hukum di mana kepentingan bersama memang perlu membatasi perilaku tertentu. Bagi saya, urutannya harus tetap jelas: hak individu adalah titik awal, lalu pembatasan hanya dibenarkan jika benar-benar perlu dan proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree that society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to silence dissent, pressure minorities, or excuse abuse by those in power, so I am very cautious about giving society the first claim. Public health, safety, and order do matter, but they should justify only narrow and proportional limits, not a blank check to override personal rights. For me, a just society is one that protects individual dignity first and builds the common good on that foundation.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, terutama ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah merasa paling tahu apa yang terbaik. Memang benar ada keadaan seperti kesehatan publik dan keselamatan yang bisa membatasi kebebasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan proporsional, bukan alasan untuk menggeser hak individu ke posisi kedua. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak individu terlebih dahulu, lalu menuntut tanggung jawab sosial di atas dasar itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still do not accept the claim that society should generally take priority over the individual. From my Indonesian perspective, “the common good” is often a convenient phrase for people in power to pressure minorities, suppress criticism, or excuse unfair treatment, so I am wary of any principle that gives the collective the first claim. Public health and safety can justify limited restrictions, but only when they are narrow, necessary, and proportional. For me, the stronger and safer principle is to protect individual rights first, because a society that ignores dignity at the personal level will eventually become unjust for everyone.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa seringnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan yang berlebihan, dan itu biasanya paling merugikan minoritas atau orang yang tidak punya suara. Memang ada keadaan seperti kesehatan publik dan keselamatan yang bisa membatasi kebebasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus benar-benar sempit, perlu, dan proporsional. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif, melainkan yang melindungi hak individu sebagai dasar lalu menuntut tanggung jawab sosial dari sana.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
